purpose: Concept-level MEP awareness for architecture planning.
safetyNote: MEP routing/load sizing/fire system design must be verified by qualified professionals. Agent should coordinate and flag requirements, not certify design.

rules: Group toilets/kitchen/utility near wet walls/shafts where possible.
Avoid routing plumbing through major living spaces where avoidable.
Provide shaft access for maintenance in multi-storey buildings.
Rainwater drainage must consider roof/terrace slopes and downpipes.
Electrical room/panel should be accessible and protected from wet areas.
Kitchen, HVAC, lift, pumps, and commercial equipment need dedicated load planning.
Avoid placing DB in toilet/wet areas.
Public/commercial projects need emergency lighting/signage planning.
AC outdoor unit placement must be planned.
Commercial spaces need HVAC zoning and ceiling/service depth coordination.
Avoid ducts crossing beams without coordination.
For high-occupancy/commercial/institutional/multi-storey projects, flag fire consultant/code compliance requirement.
Keep clear exit paths and avoid blocking exits with furniture/storage.
Plan low-voltage routes early to avoid exposed wiring.
Server/network cabinet should have ventilation and power backup if needed.
Restaurant/cloud kitchen needs stronger exhaust, wash, storage, and fire safety planning.
Domestic kitchen needs chimney/exhaust and practical appliance positions.

mepCoordinationChecks: Wet areas stacked or grouped where possible.
Shafts shown for multi-storey toilet/kitchen clusters.
Electrical panel not in wet zone.
AC outdoor units have serviceable location.
Commercial kitchen has wash, storage, exhaust, and service flow.
Fire/life-safety flags included for public/high-occupancy buildings.
